actions.thought: K8s from UTG in 6-max is a marginal hand. It's too weak to open from early position with 5 players behind me. Standard fold here to play solid poker from the start of the tournament.

I am holding Jd 2c in middle position. This is a very weak hand with poor post-flop playability and low equity. Standard strategy in a 6-max game is to fold J2o from this position, especially since I am first to act after the UTG fold. I will wait for a better spot to enter the pot.

Preflop, first hand, folds to me in the cutoff with 3d8d. This is a suited hand with poor high-card strength and significant gaps, making it unsuitable for an open-raise from cutoff in a 6-max tournament setting with 100BB stacks. Standard cutoff opening range includes stronger suited connectors like 76s+ and better broadways, but 83s lacks playability and postflop potential against likely calling ranges from button and blinds. Limping is suboptimal as it invites multiway pots with a weak hand. Best play is to fold and wait for better spots to build the stack over 150 hands.

On the button with A♠Q♣ and ~100BB effective, this is a clear open-raising hand. I want to take the initiative, extract value and apply pressure to the blinds. I’ll raise to 3x the BB (300 total) — larger than the min to discourage loose calls and to build a pot I can play in position. If called, I'll continuation-bet favorable flops and play straightforwardly (value heavily on Ace-high boards, control size on highly coordinated boards). If I face a substantial 3-bet I’ll re-evaluate based on sizing and opponent tendencies (call smaller 3-bets to realize equity, fold or tighten vs very large/Polarized 3-bets).

Facing a 3x button open from the small blind, 9♦4♦ is too weak to defend profitably out of position. Calling creates a bloated pot OOP with a hand that’s often dominated and realizes equity poorly, and turning it into a 3-bet bluff is unnecessary this early without reads (plus the big blind can squeeze). Best is to preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I am in the Big Blind with 92o, one of the weakest starting hands. The Button (Minni) has opened to 300 (3bb). While I typically defend the Big Blind widely against late-position opens, 92o has very poor equity and playability, especially out of position. The price of calling 200 into a pot of 450 requires about 31% equity, which I likely technically have raw against a wide range, but my equity realization will be terrible. This is a clear fold.

I am UTG with Ten-Trey offsuit (T3o), which is a very weak hand. In a 6-max game, opening such a wide and weak range from the earliest position is mathematically incorrect and will lead to significant losses. The best play is to fold and wait for a better starting hand.

Preflop in middle position with 83o, a very weak offsuit non-connected hand. UTG folded, no action yet, but this hand has no playability postflop and is not worth entering the pot voluntarily from MP in a 6-handed game. Standard fold to preserve stack early in tournament.

Cutoff with UTG and MP folded — in 6-max this is a position to open a fairly wide stealing range. J7o is marginal but playable as a late-position open to take the blinds or isolate weaker hands. I will raise rather than limp (limping gives cheap multiway pots and surrenders initiative). I pick a slightly larger open (3bb = 300) instead of the minimum 2bb to gain more fold equity and discourage loose calls from the blinds. If I get a large 3-bet I will fold and preserve my deep stack; if I get single calls I’ll use position to play postflop and c-bet appropriate textures.

CO opens to 3bb and we’re on the Button with K6s. This hand is a bit dominated when we make top pair, but suited + position gives us enough playability to defend without bloating the pot. A 3-bet here is possible as a bluff, but early with no reads and decent squeeze risk from the blinds, calling keeps ranges wide and lets us realize equity in position; if we face a squeeze we can comfortably fold.

I hold 2c 9s (92o) in the Small Blind. This is one of the weakest starting hands in poker. Facing a raise from the Cutoff and a call from the Button, I am out of position against two players showing strength. Although the pot odds are roughly 4:1, the poor playability of the hand and lack of equity make calling -EV. Folding is the only standard option.

4-6 offsuit is a weak hand. Even with decent pot odds (~3.75:1), this hand plays poorly multiway and out of position. No straight potential, not suited, and will often be dominated or make weak pairs. Better to fold and wait for a better spot.

I opened from the cutoff and the button called, so heads-up my continuation-range is strong here (Qs, 5x, many spades and broadways). The flop Qs 5s 5d gives me no pair or flush chance with Jc7d, so I have little showdown value — this is a pure c-bet bluff spot to apply pressure. A bet of ~2/3 pot (500 into 750) credibly represents Qx/5x and charges spade draws, folding many hands in the caller’s wide calling range. Plan: bet 500 to take the pot now; fold to a significant raise (they'll often have a made hand or the nut draw). If called, re-evaluate on the turn and proceed cautiously.

Paired Q55 is a relatively static flop where CO can c-bet a wide range (overpairs, Qx, spade draws, and a lot of unpaired broadways/A-high). With K6cc we have weak immediate equity (no spade, no pair), but we do block some KQ, we’re in position, and we can realize equity + potentially win later when Minni gives up on turns. Plan: call once, then continue only on improving/equity turns (K/6/club turns) or if checked to (stab); fold versus a strong turn barrel on bricks.

I opened preflop and c-bet the flop as a bluff; Villain called that bet and now checked the turn (8c), which looks like weakness. I hold Jc7d with no pair and no spade draw, so my only realistic path to win this pot is fold equity. The 8c is a blank for most of Villain's calling range (Qx, 5x, small pairs, spade draws), so a credible turn bet can fold out many marginal hands. I will make a ~2/3-pot second barrel (1200 into 1750) — large enough to apply pressure and deny equity to spade draws, but small relative to my 9350 stack. Plan: bet 1200 to take the pot; if I get a significant raise I will fold (no showdown value); if called I’ll re-evaluate on the river.

Turn barrel into a paired board after we called flop is heavily weighted toward value (Qx/overpairs/5x) plus some spade-draw bluffs. With Kc6c we have essentially no draw equity and only 6 outs to a weak pair that’s often dominated and can create reverse implied odds. Pot odds require ~29% equity, which we don’t have, and we don’t hold key spade blockers to justify a float-and-bluff line. Best play is to cut losses and preserve stack.

UTG in 6-handed play with 100BB stacks on hand 3/150. TJo offsuit is outside standard RFI range (typically 22+, A9s+, KTs+, QJs+, AJo+, KQo+). Vulnerable to squeezes from aggressive Minni in MP and action behind from CO/BTN. Folding preserves stack for better spots early in tournament.

I hold A♥Q♦ (AQ offsuit) in middle position with a 111 BB stack. AQo is a strong open-raising hand — it wants to take the initiative preflop and isolate the blinds/late positions. A standard 3x BB open (300 total) achieves value and fold equity while discouraging multiway callers; a min-raise to 200 is too small and invites too many callers. Plan: raise to 300; if called I'll evaluate the flop and continue with c-bets on favorable textures. If I face a large 3-bet from a tight opponent I'll often fold, but may call smaller 3-bets depending on opponent and sizing.

Facing a 3bb open from middle position, 7♠6♦ offsuit is too weak to continue profitably. It has poor playability (no suit), makes dominated pairs/weak straights, and doesn’t have good blocker value to justify a light 3-bet. Even with position, the required equity versus a solid opening range and the higher open size make this a clear fold.

I am holding 5c 6s (56 offsuit) on the Button. An opponent in Middle Position (Minni) has raised to 3bb. While I have position, 56o is a very weak hand with poor playability and equity against a standard opening range. I am not getting the right immediate odds to call (requiring about 40% equity against the pot odds, while possessing significantly less), and calling invites a squeeze from the blinds. There is no strategic merit in entering this pot with this holding.

K2o is a very weak hand to defend from the small blind against a 3x open from middle position. Even though Minni has been aggressive (raising both previous hands), K2o is dominated by most of their value range and plays poorly postflop. Folding is the clear play here.

I am in the Big Blind holding Jack-Ten offsuit. Minni has opened for 300 from Middle position, which is the third consecutive hand they have raised, indicating a potentially wide or aggressive opening range. I need to put in 200 more chips to call a pot that currently has 450. The pot odds (2.25-to-1) are sufficient to defend my blind with a hand like JTo, despite being out of position for the rest of the hand. I will call to see a flop and evaluate from there.

I am first to act on a flop of 8c-7c-5s. My hand, Jack-Ten offsuit, provides me with two overcards but no immediate draw other than a backdoor straight. The board is quite connected and favors a range that could have sets, two-pair, or strong straight draws. Given that Minni has been the aggressor in every hand so far, I expect a continuation bet. Checking is the standard play here to see how she proceeds and to avoid bloating the pot with a marginal holding out of position.

I opened to 300 preflop with AQo and was called by the big blind. Flop 8c-7c-5s is fairly dry and advantages the preflop raiser — many of the BB’s wide calling combos miss this board. I have two overcards (including the Ace) with no club, so I don’t have a made hand but I have decent showdown value and fold equity as the aggressor. Standard play is a continuation bet to take the pot now and to deny equity to club draws and unpaired hands. I’ll size modestly (~1/3 pot) to 250 to pick up folds and avoid bloating the pot with just Ace-high. If I face a large raise I’ll give this up; if called I’ll re-evaluate on the turn and barrel selectively on safe cards or check behind to control the pot versus heavy action.

I am getting 3.6-to-1 pot odds (250 to call into a 900 pot) to see the turn. While I only have Jack-high, I have two overcards and a backdoor straight draw (a 9 on the turn gives me an open-ended straight draw). Minni has raised every hand so far, which suggests her range is likely wider than average and her continuation bet could be a standard play with many hands I currently flip with or trail slightly. Given the favorable odds and my potential outs, calling to see the turn is a profitable play.

I am first to act on the turn with the board showing 8c-7c-5s-4d. My hand is currently Jack-high with a gutshot straight draw to a Nine. The board is extremely coordinated and connects well with a wide range of hands, especially those containing a Six which would have completed a straight. Given Minni's persistent aggression across all three hands played so far, I expect another bet. I will check to evaluate her bet sizing and determine if the pot odds justify chasing my gutshot or if my overcards still have any value.

I hold A♥Q♦ and c-bet the dry 8c-7c-5s flop (250) and was called. The turn 4♦ does not improve my hand but actually helps villain’s range: any 6x now has a made straight, pocket pairs remain ahead, and club backdoors still have one-card outs. With only Ace-high and limited fold equity versus a calling range that contains many hands that now beat me, the prudent, low-variance line is to check and control the pot (1150). Checking lets me realize equity cheaply, avoids getting raised off marginal hands, and keeps options open to bluff or call on the river depending on the card and villain’s line.

On the river, the Nine of Clubs completes a Jack-high straight for me (7-8-9-T-J). This is a very strong hand, although the board now also shows three clubs, making a flush possible. Minni checked back on the turn, which suggests she might have been slowing down with a marginal hand or a draw. Since I've made the high end of the straight, I need to bet for value. I'll bet roughly half the pot (600 chips) to extract value from sets, overpairs, or lower straights (any 6) that Minni might hold, while still being able to fold or evaluate if she raises significantly, which would represent a flush.

I opened MP with A♦Q♥ and c-bet the dry flop; BB (Flash) called. I checked the turn to control the pot and get to showdown cheaply with only Ace‑high. The river 9♣ completes a couple important parts of his range: any single 6 in his hand made the straight on the 4♦ turn, and the 9♣ gives value to combos that pair the river (or now make trips with pocket 9s). More importantly, only players holding two clubs (both hole cards clubs) make a club flush on the river — and his passive call on the flop + check on the turn line can easily hide a lot of these value combos (6x straights, pocket 7/8/5, 9x, and the occasional two‑club combo). He leads out a small bet (600 into a 1750 pot) which gives me ~25.5% equity to profitably call. My Ace‑high has very little showdown value against his likely value-heavy range and I do not block the most dangerous combos (I hold no clubs). Raising as a bluff is risky — I would be representing hands that beat me and would face calls from many real value hands. Given the pot odds and villain’s line, I expect his bluff frequency here to be well under the ~26% needed, so the lowest-variance, +EV play is to fold.

Utg in a 6-handed game with 3♠5♠ is too weak to invest chips preflop. The pot is 150 and it costs 100 to call, but I’d be out of position against five players and this hand is often dominated by higher spades or overcards; its straight/flush potential is poor in multiway action. With deep stacks I should be selective and look for better spots — limp/calling will lead to uncomfortable postflop decisions and a small raise is a thin bluff that’s out of position. Fold and conserve chips for a more profitable opportunity.

4♠2♣ is the weakest offsuit combo with terrible playability and reverse implied odds. In middle position with several players left to act (including button and blinds), limping is -EV and raising is unnecessary risk. Preserve stack and wait for better spots.

I hold 4h 8d in the Cutoff. This is a very weak hand (low, unsuited, disconnected). With two players folding to me, I still have the Button and both Blinds to act behind me. Opening with this hand is not profitable in the long run as it has poor playability and low equity against any calling range. The standard and correct play is to fold.

On the button with T7o and it's folded to me. This is a good spot to steal the blinds with position. T7o is playable enough on the button, and I'll have position postflop if called. Standard raise to 250.

I am in the Small Blind holding 2d6s, which is an extremely weak hand. Claude has raised from the Button, which is a standard position for a wide opening range, but 62 offsuit is not worth defending even against a wide steal attempt, especially while being out of position for the remainder of the hand. I will fold and wait for a better spot.

Pocket 5s is a premium hand preflop, especially against a button raise after multiple folds, which is likely a wide stealing range including Ax suited, broadways, small pairs, and suited connectors. Calling OOP gives poor postflop playability with a set-mining spot (pot odds 400:150 ~2.7:1 direct, but need implied odds). 3-betting builds the pot when ahead and can take it down preflop. Sizing to 750 (3x the raise) is standard, applies pressure, and with deep stacks (~100BB), we can continue postflop if called. Folding or flat-calling too weak.

Facing a 3-bet from the BB with T7o. This is a clear fold - T7o has poor equity against a 3-betting range and lacks the playability (no suited cards, no connected cards) to continue. Even with position, calling 500 more with this hand is unprofitable.

6-max and ~100bb deep, K8s is a borderline but playable UTG open. I prefer raising (not limping) to take initiative, pressure the blinds, and realize equity with a suited hand that can make strong draws. Using a standard 2.5x size keeps ranges consistent and reduces multiway pots out of position.

I am holding 7h 6d in Middle Position facing a raise from the Under-the-Gun (UTG) player to 250. UTG ranges are typically strong, consisting of pocket pairs and high broadway cards. My hand, an unsuited connector, has poor equity against this range and is vulnerable to domination. Additionally, there are four players left to act behind me, increasing the risk of a squeeze or a call from a better hand. Calling or 3-betting here would be loose and negative EV. The prudent play is to fold.

A4o in the cutoff facing a UTG raise to 2.5x. UTG raises typically represent stronger ranges. A4 offsuit is a marginal hand that doesn't play well against an early position raising range - it's dominated by better aces and doesn't have strong postflop playability. Folding is the disciplined play here.

I am on the button holding Ace-Trey suited (A3s). This is a strong speculative hand that plays very well in position. FiveTwo has raised from UTG, which typically indicates a strong range. Calling here is a standard play to utilize my positional advantage post-flop and keep the pot manageable while retaining the potential to hit the nut flush or a wheel straight. I prefer calling over 3-betting in this specific spot to see a flop cheaply, though I must be cautious of a potential squeeze from the aggressive players in the blinds, Elon and Minni.

A9o is a strong 3-bet candidate from the SB facing an UTG raise to 2.5BB and a button call. UTG range is relatively tight but has significant fold equity to a 3-bet. The button call is likely wide, folding much of its range. Ace provides good blocker value and equity when called. Deep stacks (100BB+) allow for good post-flop playability if continued. Aggression helps build stack early in the tournament. Sizing to 900 (about 3.6x the open raise, accounting for dead money and multiway pot) balances value and bluff frequencies. Post-flop position as SB is second-to-last to act if checked around.

Jd8s is a weak, offsuit hand with poor playability. Facing an UTG open and a button cold-call, Elon’s large isolation 3-bet to 900 represents a strong or aggressive isolating range — I’d need roughly 800/(1500+800) ≈ 34.8% equity to justify a call, which J8o won’t have vs those ranges, especially out of position and likely multiway. A min 4-bet to 1550 would be a thin, high-variance bluff and unnecessary early in the tournament. Preserve chips and fold.

We opened a marginal K8s UTG and now face a small blind squeeze 3-bet with the button already having shown interest (cold-called). Against a typically strong SB 3-bet/squeeze range (AK/AQ, KQ, big pairs, suited broadways), K8s is frequently dominated and performs poorly out of position in an inflated pot, with significant reverse implied odds when we make top pair. A 4-bet bluff is also unattractive here since our blocker value is modest and the SB’s range is strong. Best EV is to cut losses and preserve the stack.

Elon has squeezed from the Small Blind after FiveTwo's initial raise and my call. With the original raiser now folding, I am facing a 3-bet of 900 total. I need to call 650 more into a pot that will be 2150. A3s is a strong speculative hand with nut potential (flush and wheel straight possibilities) and plays exceptionally well in position. Given the deep stacks (100BB) and the fact that I have absolute position on Elon for the remainder of the hand, calling to see a flop is the most profitable play. I can capitalize on my positional advantage to realize my equity or take the pot away on favorable boards.

Preflop 3-bet with A9o got called by button, who has a wide range including Ax, broadway, suited connectors, pairs. Flop Td9s5c gives me middle pair with top kicker (pair of 9s, A kicker) and Ace blocker reducing his Ax strong hands. Board is somewhat drawy (gutshot possibilities like J8, QJ, straight draws) but rainbow suits limit flush draws. As preflop aggressor in positionally advantageous spot (SB vs BTN heads-up), continuation bet extracts value from his worse Ax/pairs/draws and denies equity to floats/draws. Pot 2150, bet sizing to ~60% pot (1300) balances value/bluff frequencies, keeps pot manageable with deep stacks (~90BB), and sets up turn play.

On a Td 9s 5c flop, Elon has led for 1300 into a 2150 pot after 3-betting from the small blind pre-flop. My hand, Ad 3d, has missed this flop significantly, offering only a backdoor nut flush draw (with the Td on board) and an overcard that may not even be clean given Elon's strong 3-betting range (JJ+, AK, AQ). The board texture favors a typical 3-betting range which includes overpairs like JJ, QQ, KK, and AA, as well as sets like TT or 99. Calling 1300 (roughly 60% pot) with just Ace-high and a backdoor draw is not mathematically sound, as I lack the immediate equity to continue against such a sizing. Folding allows me to preserve my stack for more favorable spots.

I am in UTG position with AJs, which is a strong starting hand in a 6-handed game. I have been tight so far (folded first 5 hands), so an open raise here commands respect. I will raise to 2.5bb (250) to build the pot for value while maintaining a standard opening size that balances my range.

65 suited is a speculative hand. Facing UTG raise to 250 with 4 players behind who could squeeze. While suited connectors can flop well, this is a marginal spot in middle position. The risk of a 3-bet behind and poor position post-flop makes this a fold.

I am holding 7d 4h in the cutoff facing a 2.5x raise from Pro in UTG. 74o is one of the weakest starting hands and has no business continuing against an early-position open. The hand has poor post-flop playability and lacks the equity to justify a call or a 3-bet bluff here.

Q7 suited on the button facing an UTG raise to 250 into a 400 pot. Pro has folded every previous hand preflop (5/5), suggesting a tight range for their first raise UTG, likely premium hands like pairs, broadway, suited aces. Q7s is speculative with flush potential but gappy, poor equity vs tight UTG range (~30-40% at best heads-up). Deep stacks but position alone doesn't justify calling given the strength disparity. SB and BB still to act, but no fold equity without raising. Best play is to fold and wait for better spots to build stack.

UTG (Pro) opened to 250 from early position — a fairly strong range in 6-handed. I’m in the small blind with T♠2♣ (offsuit Ten-Deuce): a weak, dominated hand and I’ll be out of position for the rest of the street. Pot is 400 and it costs me 200 to call, so I’d be getting 2:1 pot odds (need ~33% equity). Versus a standard UTG opening range my equity with T2o is well below that, and 3-betting or completing to 400 is unjustified (thin bluff and risk of getting further committed out of position). With a healthy stack (≈104bb) I can wait for a better spot — fold and preserve chips.

We’re in the BB with KK versus an UTG open in a 6-max game. This is a premium value hand that wants to build the pot immediately, deny equity, and isolate the opener. Flatting keeps in dominated hands but also invites awkward multiway dynamics and lets UTG realize equity too easily; 3-betting is the highest EV line. Out of position, I’ll use a slightly larger size than in-position: raise to 900 (about 3.6x the open), which pressures UTG’s opening range and sets up comfortable stack-off on many runouts. If UTG 4-bets, KK is strong enough to continue aggressively (often committing stacks).

I opened UTG with AJs, a strong hand, and received a 3-bet from the Big Blind. While the 3-bet indicates strength, especially against an UTG open from a player who has been tight, AJs is too strong to fold immediately. I will have position on the Big Blind post-flop, which is a significant advantage. 4-betting would likely isolate me against a range that dominates me (QQ+, AK), whereas calling allows me to keep their range wider and realize equity in position. The pot odds require me to call 650 to play for a pot of 1850, needing roughly 35% equity, which AJs has against a reasonable 3-betting range.

3-bet pot OOP vs UTG caller on an A♠T♠5♠ monotone flop. KK is now a medium-strength bluff-catcher (often behind any Ax and all made flushes), but the K♠ is an important blocker that reduces villain’s strongest flush/flush-draw combos and gives us credible range advantage (we can have AA/AK/AQ and strong spade holdings). I’ll use a small c-bet to apply pressure to pocket pairs/broadways without a spade, deny equity, and keep the pot manageable. If we face a raise (especially larger sizing), we can comfortably fold given how value-heavy raises are on monotone boards.

I hold Top Pair (Aces) with a Jack kicker on a monotone spade board (As Ts 5s). I do not hold a spade. The opponent (BB) 3-bet pre-flop and is now betting small (450 into 1850, roughly 25% pot). Given the pre-flop strength shown by the 3-bet, their range includes strong Aces (AK, AQ), sets (AA, TT), and flushes, but also hands I beat like KK or QQ (possibly with a spade) or semi-bluffs (KsQx). The small bet size offers me excellent pot odds (roughly 6 to 1). Folding Top Pair to a 1/4 pot bet is too nitty, but raising isolates me against flushes and better Aces. I will call to re-evaluate on the turn, keeping their bluffing range in.

Turn is a relative brick (9h) after we c-bet small on the A♠T♠5♠ monotone flop and got called. In a 3-bet pot vs an UTG continue, villain’s range is now fairly Ax-heavy plus spade holdings (often one-spade hands that are drawing, and some strong made hands). Firing again with KK (even with K♠) doesn’t fold out the hands that beat us (Ax/flushes/sets) often enough, and we risk getting put in a nasty spot vs a raise on a board where raises are very value-dense. Checking keeps the pot manageable, lets us realize equity, and allows villain to bluff (especially with one-spade hands) while we can call reasonable sizes with K♠ as an important blocker and re-evaluate rivers (notably, a spade river gives us the nut flush with K♠).

I hold Top Pair (Aces) with a Jack kicker on a dangerous board (As Ts 5s 9h). The opponent (FiveTwo) 3-bet pre-flop and c-bet small on the flop, representing a strong range that includes flushes, sets, and strong Ax hands. Their check on the turn could be a trap with a flush or pot control with a hand like AK, AQ, or KK with a spade. Since I do not hold a spade, I am vulnerable to a river spade, but betting here likely only gets called by hands that beat me (AK, AQ, flushes) or strong draws. Betting folds out the hands I beat (KK/QQ without a spade). By checking back, I control the size of the pot, avoid getting check-raised off my equity, and can re-evaluate on the river, possibly inducing bluffs or getting to showdown cheaply.

River brings the 4th spade and pairs the 9, giving us the K♠-high flush, which is the nut flush on this board (A♠ is on board, so no higher flush exists). Pro’s turn check-back caps a lot of his range to Ax (often without a spade), medium pairs with/without a spade, and some one-spade hands that now made a worse flush. We should lead for value rather than check and risk a check-back from those hands. Because the board is paired, a raise from Pro is heavily weighted toward boats/quads (A9/TT/99/55, etc.), so we use a sizing that gets called by worse while keeping our bet/fold clean versus aggression.

The river brings a fourth spade (As Ts 5s 9s) and pairs the board (9h 9s). I hold AdJd, giving me Two Pair (Aces and Nines) with a Jack kicker, but I hold no spade. In a 3-bet pot, the opponent's range is heavy on broadway cards (AK, AQ, KQs) and pocket pairs (KK, QQ, TT). Given the 4-flush board, any spade in the opponent's hand makes a flush. Additionally, with the board paired, I lose to any Full House (sets or two pair filling up) and even to better two pairs like AK, AQ, KK, and QQ (which play as AA-99-K/Q vs my AA-99-J). I essentially beat nothing but a total air bluff with no spade, which is unlikely given the line. The probability that the Big Blind 3-bettor has a spade, a full house, or a better kicker is overwhelmingly high. Folding is the only prudent option.
43 suited from UTG is too weak to open. This hand has very little equity and poor playability from the worst position at the table. Easy fold.
Q3 offsuit is a very weak hand to play from middle position. It has poor equity and lacks connectivity or high-card strength. Folding is the standard and correct play in this spot.

Folded to me in the cutoff position with Kd7s. This is a standard stealing spot with a wide opening range from late position. K7o has decent king-high equity and fold equity against blinds and button. Opponents have shown aggression but deep stacks favor position. Standard raise size of 2.5x BB to build pot or take it down preflop. No strong blockers or reasons to limp/fold.

On the button with pocket Jacks facing a cutoff open to 250 (2.5x). JJ is a clear value 3-bet candidate here: I want to isolate the opener, gain fold equity, and build a pot playing in position. A raise to 750 (~3x the open) is large enough to charge callers in the blinds and extract value from worse hands (AQ/AK, mid pairs) but not so large that I overcommit versus a polarized 4-bet. Plan: if called, C‑bet favorable dry flops and pot-control or check back on coordinated high-card/wet boards; if I face a sizable 4-bet/shove I’ll re-evaluate (fold to a polarizing shove or call only against small, non‑polarized 4-bets).

We’re in the small blind facing a cutoff open and a button 3-bet, and we’ll be out of position for the rest of the hand with the big blind still to act. 8c5c has decent playability in single-raised pots, but cold-calling a 3-bet OOP without strong equity, blockers, or clear implied-odds (and with squeeze risk from the BB) is a losing spot. A 4-bet bluff is also poor here due to minimal blocker value. Best decision is to fold and preserve stack for better positions/spots.

I am holding 2c 9s in the Big Blind, which is a very weak hand. The action involves a raise from the Cutoff and a 3-bet from the Button. Facing a 3-bet with 72o-tier trash is a trivial fold. I have no equity to justify calling 650 chips into a pot of 1150, especially out of position against two players showing strength.

Facing a 3-bet to 750 from Minni on the button after my 250 open from cutoff with Kd7s. Pot 1150, to call 500. K7s has marginal equity against a typical button 3-bet range (JJ+, AK, some suited broadways and bluffs), likely around 20-30% depending on polarization, but postflop I'd be first to act (effectively OOP since blinds folded). Minni has been aggressive with opens but this is her first 3-bet shown. Deep stacks (~100BB), early in tournament (hand 7), no need to get involved with a mediocre hand lacking strong playability or blockers for a 4-bet bluff. Pot odds are ~3:1 but equity and positional disadvantage make calling -EV. 4-betting risks too much for fold equity against an aggressive player. Standard fold to preserve stack.

King-Seven suited is a marginal hand in early position (UTG). In a 6-handed game, this is slightly below the standard opening range for this position. Given that players like Minni and Elon have shown significant pre-flop aggression in earlier hands, I risk being 3-bet or forced to play a multi-way pot out of position with a hand that often finds itself dominated. I will fold and wait for a more favorable situation.

Folded to me in middle position with K9o, a decent speculative hand with king blocker. Deep stacks (~100BB), early in tournament. Standard play is to open-raise for value/steal blinds vs tight blinds (Pro and Claude have folded/showed weakness in spots). Minni in CO is aggressive but K9o has good playability postflop. Raise to 3x BB (300 total) to build pot and take initiative. Folding too tight, limping poor strategy.

Qs Jh (QJo) in the cutoff facing a 3x open to 300 from middle. I have ~108 BB and I have position on the opener postflop. QJo is a playable, well-connected broadway hand but not strong enough for a standard 3-bet for value here; a min-raise to 500 would be too thin and can invite squeezes/4-bets. Calling keeps the pot controlled, keeps weaker hands in, and lets me use positional advantage to realize equity or apply pressure on favorable flops. I’ll call and reassess on the flop (continue with top pair/strong draws, fold to heavy action with nothing).

Facing an MP open to 300 and a cutoff flat, 5c6d is too weak/off-suit to profitably continue even on the button. It has poor playability versus two relatively strong ranges, makes dominated/fragile straights, and doesn’t 3-bet well as a squeeze bluff given the caller. Preserve chips for better spots.

I hold 4s 2d in the Small Blind, which is one of the weakest hands in poker. Facing a raise from Elon in middle position and a call from Minni in the cutoff, I am getting 3:1 pot odds, but my equity is well below the required 25%. Furthermore, I would be playing the rest of the hand out of position against two opponents with a hand that has very low playability and reverse implied odds. Folding is the only correct play here.

I have 78o in the BB with pot odds of ~3.75:1 (calling 200 into 750). This is a connected hand that can make straights and I'm closing the action. With these pot odds and position to act last preflop, calling is standard.

I flopped middle pair (8s) with a weak kicker on J-8-5 board. Against Elon's preflop raise and Minni's call, either could easily have a Jack or an overpair. My hand has showdown value but isn't strong enough to build a big pot. Checking to see what develops and pot control is the right play here.

Preflop raised K9o from MP as planned for value/steal with good playability and deep stacks. Flop 8h5dJc is a complete miss: king-high no pair, no straight draw (gutters unlikely), weak heart flush draw (one card to 4 hearts). Board is fairly dry rainbow J-high. 3 players to the flop (Minni CO caller, Claude BB defender). Acting second postflop after BB check, OOP. Pot 950 (~9.5BB), deep effective stacks ~100BB. No showdown value long-term vs villain ranges (Minni: broadway, Ax, pairs/Jx; Claude: wide calling range including Jx, 87-suited, draws). C-betting air here multiway OOP risks bloating pot, facing calls/raises from better hands/draws without fold equity (Minni aggressive). Better to check back, pot control, realize equity with king-high blocker (blocks KQo/KJs), take free turn card, and reassess. Avoid spewing early in tournament.

I have top pair (Jx) with a good queen kicker (QsJh) on a dry, rainbow 8-5-J flop and I’m in position vs two opponents who both checked. This is a good spot to bet for value and deny free cards to sets/overpairs or overcards. A half‑pot lead (~475 into 950) extracts from worse Jx and Qx hands, keeps weaker holdings paying to see a turn, and still leaves room to fold/call if I face significant aggression. If I’m raised big I’ll strongly consider folding (sets/strong Jx likely); if I get a single caller I’ll reassess on the turn and proceed cautiously on dangerous cards.

I have middle pair (8s) with a weak kicker on J-8-5 board. Minni bet 475 into ~950, giving me 3:1 pot odds (need ~25% equity). My middle pair definitely has enough equity to call against Minni's range which includes Jx, draws, and bluffs. Elon checked showing weakness. Folding is too tight with these odds, raising doesn't make sense with showdown value. Calling to see the turn.

Preflop raiser with K9o, checked flop for pot control on a miss (king-high, no pair/draws on J85 rainbow board). Minni (aggressive CO caller pre) now donk-bets 475 into 3-way checked pot (~950 pre pot?), Claude calls. Multiway facing bet+call OOP deep (~100BB), my hand has zero equity (no pair/straight/flush draw), poor blockers, no fold equity to raise bluff. King-high dead vs villain ranges (Minni: Jx/pairs/Ax/draws; Claude: wide defenders like Jx, suited connectors, weak pairs). Pot odds ~4:1 but implied odds negative multiway without hand. Standard fold to avoid spew early tournament, preserve stack for better spots. Realize checked back plan when facing strength.

Turn brought the 4h, adding a heart flush draw. I still have middle pair with a weak kicker. Heads-up against Minni who showed aggression on the flop. My hand has showdown value but isn't strong enough to build a pot - I'll get called by better and fold out worse if I bet. Check for pot control and see what Minni does.

I have top pair (J) with a strong queen kicker (QsJh) and I'm in position vs the single caller (Claude) who check‑called my flop bet. The turn 4h is a largely irrelevant card — it doesn't complete a flush (no player can make a 5‑card heart flush this street) and it doesn't give obvious two‑pair combos that weren't already possible. Betting here is the right line to extract value from worse Jx, Qx and medium pairs, and to deny free cards that could outdraw me on the river. I want a size that gets called by these worse hands but still gives me room to fold to significant aggression from a set/two‑pair. I'll make a standard half‑pot value/protection bet (~950 into 1900). If I face a large raise I'll strongly consider folding; if called I'll re‑evaluate on the river.

I have middle pair (8s) with a 7 kicker. Minni bet 950 into 2850, giving me about 3:1 pot odds (~25% equity needed). Minni's line shows strength - called preflop, bet flop, bet turn. They likely have Jx or possibly an overpair, but could also have draws (heart flush draw) or semi-bluffs. My middle pair has showdown value and the pot odds are decent. Folding is too tight here; calling to see the river and re-evaluate.

River completed a possible heart flush (3 hearts on board). I have middle pair (8s) with weak kicker. The pot is 3800 and it's checked to me. My hand has showdown value but isn't strong enough to bet for value - I'd only get called by better hands. Checking to see if Minni checks back or bets. If they bet, I'll need to decide based on sizing whether my middle pair is good.

I have top pair (J) with a strong Q kicker and I was the aggressor on flop and turn, getting one passive caller (Claude) both streets. The river 3h completes a three‑heart board (8h-5d-Jc-4h-3h), which opens the possibility of two‑heart flushes in his range. Claude’s line (check-call, check-call, then check on the river) looks passive/pot‑control rather than aggressive. Betting here would extract thin value from worse Jx or second pairs but also risks a check‑raise from sets/two‑pair/flushes and is vulnerable to folding out some hands I beat. Given the completed heart draw and his passive line, the lowest‑risk, highest‑EV line is to check and go to showdown — realize equity and avoid getting exploited by a river raise. I’ll check and show down.

UTG position in a 6-handed game with deep stacks (~110BB). Q9o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability postflop, dominated by better queens and lacking suited/connectivity for speculation. Standard UTG opening range excludes this hand. Folding preserves stack for premium spots early in tournament (hand 9/150). No relevant opponent reads yet to justify limping or raising.

2♦4♠ is a very weak, disconnected offsuit with almost no postflop playability. The pot is 150 and it costs 100 to call, so I need ~40% equity to break even — 2-4 offsuit is well under that against even a single random hand and becomes much worse multiway. I’m in middle position with multiple players left to act behind (Cutoff, Button, SB, BB) who can call or reraise, so calling or min-raising would likely lead to multiway action or facing stronger ranges. No profitable reason to invest chips here — preserve my stack for better spots and fold.

Fold. 82o is too weak to open from the cutoff even in 6-max: it’s frequently dominated when called, has poor postflop playability, and doesn’t realize equity well. Limping for 100 invites multiway spots with a hand that can’t profitably continue, and raising as a steal is unnecessary against two blinds that can defend/3-bet. Preserve stack and wait for better steal candidates.

I am on the Button with Jack-Ten suited (Jd Td), a very strong hand for this position due to its playability and potential to make straights and flushes. The action has folded to me, giving me an excellent opportunity to steal the blinds or play a pot in position. A standard raise size of 2.5BB (250 chips) is appropriate to apply pressure to the blinds while building a pot with a hand that performs well post-flop.

I have AKo in the small blind - a premium hand. Pro opened to 250 from the button which is often a wide steal attempt. I should 3-bet for value here. A standard 3-bet to around 800-900 applies pressure and builds the pot with my strong holding.

I am in the Big Blind with King-Four offsuit (Kd 4h). Pro (Button) opened for 250 and Claude (Small Blind) 3-bet to 850. King-Four offsuit is a weak hand and is significantly behind the likely ranges of both the original raiser and the 3-better. Calling would put me out of position in a bloated pot with a hand that has poor playability and equity. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here.

I hold Jack-Ten suited (Jd Td) on the Button and face a 3-bet to 850 from Claude in the Small Blind. This is a premium suited connector that plays very well in position against 3-bet ranges. I need to call 600 chips to win a total pot of 1800 (post-call), giving me 3:1 pot odds (33% equity required). JTs has solid equity and playability against a strong range. Folding is too tight given my position and hand strength, and 4-betting would likely force me to fold to a jam, wasting the hand's post-flop potential. Therefore, calling to see a flop in position is the optimal play.

Flop is 777 - three sevens on board. I have AKo which gives me trip 7s with the best possible unpaired kickers. The only hands that beat me are pocket pairs (giving full houses) or the case 7 (quads). Pro called my 3-bet, so they could have mid-high pocket pairs. I should c-bet for value here - if they have a pocket pair they'll likely call/raise, and AK high kicker is strong otherwise. Betting around 55% pot.

Pre-flop, I called a 3-bet in position with JTs, which is standard. The flop comes 7-7-7. This board texture interacts poorly with both ranges, but the opponent's range (likely consisting of high pairs 88+, AK, AQ) maintains its pre-flop equity advantage. Claude's continuation bet of 1000 into 1800 (over 50% pot) is sizable and suggests strength or a desire to deny equity to floats. With only Jack-high, no immediate draws, and practically no backdoor flush potential (requiring runner-runner diamonds), my equity against a standard 3-bet range (even just AK/AQ) is poor (around 25-30%). Floating here without a pair or draw is loose play against this sizing. The prudent move is to release the hand.

UTG with J♦6♥ — a weak, offsuit, poorly-connected hand that plays badly out of position. There are five players left to act and we've seen several opponents open/3-bet frequently in earlier hands, so calling 100 or attempting an open-raise would put me in tough postflop spots without equity or kicker protection. With deep stacks I can wait for a hand I can play aggressively; folding preserves my stack and table image. Standard strategy: fold junk from UTG in 6-handed play.

7♠3♠ is far too weak to open or limp from middle position with multiple players left to act. It has poor high-card value, is often dominated when it makes a pair, and doesn’t realize equity well without position/initiative. Best to preserve chips for better steal/defend spots.

I am in the Cutoff with Ten-Six offsuit (6d Tc). This is a very weak hand with poor playability (unsuited, gapped). Although it is folded to me, opening this hand is generally negative EV, especially with three players (Button, SB, BB) left to act who cover me or have significant stacks. At this early stage of the tournament (Hand 10/150), there is no need to take unnecessary risks with junk hands. The correct play is to fold.
Button with K5o and it folds to me. This is a clear steal spot against just the blinds. K5o is easily good enough to raise here for a steal attempt. Standard 2.5x raise.

I am in the Small Blind with J9 offsuit. Claude has raised from the Button. While J9o is a decent hand, it performs poorly out of position, especially when facing a raise. Calling would put me in a difficult spot for the rest of the hand, being first to act on every street, and it also invites the Big Blind (Elon) to squeeze or call. Given the early stage of the tournament and the disadvantageous position, the most disciplined play is to fold and wait for better opportunities.

Pocket 9s is a premium hand, well ahead of a button opener's wide range, which includes broadway cards, suited connectors, Ax suited, and weaker pairs. Facing a standard 2.5x button raise with position on only one opponent left to act (none, since SB folded), 3-betting is optimal for value, isolation, and denying equity to weaker hands. Flatting OOP risks playing out of position post-flop against a wide range. Sizing to 800 (about 3.2x the raise) is standard, building a pot while keeping fold equity. Stacks are deep enough (109 BB effective), but this spot doesn't warrant shoving. Min raise is too small.

Facing a 3-bet from Elon in the BB after my button steal attempt. K5o is a weak hand that doesn't play well enough to continue against a 3-bet, even with position. I need to call 550 into 1100 (2:1 odds), but K5o has poor equity against a 3-betting range and will be difficult to navigate postflop. Elon also 3-bet me in hand 4 and I folded then too. This is a standard fold.

6-handed from UTG, JTc suited is comfortably within a solid open-raising range due to its connectivity, nutted-flush potential, and good playability in position on later streets. With ~100bb effective stacks, we want to enter the pot with initiative rather than limp/call and invite multiway pots. I’ll use a standard 2.5bb open to pressure blinds and keep my range consistent.

I am holding Ace-Five offsuit (Ac5s) in middle position facing a raise from the Under the Gun (UTG) player, FiveTwo. A UTG raise typically signifies a strong range (pairs, broadway cards, strong aces). A5o is generally dominated by a UTG opening range and plays poorly post-flop due to lack of connectivity and kicker problems. With 75 big blinds effective, calling would expose me to reverse implied odds and squeezes from players yet to act. Three-betting is generally reserved for suited wheel aces (A5s) rather than offsuit ones in this configuration. Therefore, the disciplined and standard play is to fold.
T5o is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability. Facing an UTG raise (typically a strong range), this hand has little equity and doesn't play well postflop. Easy fold from the cutoff.

I am on the button with 7-2 offsuit, which is the weakest possible starting hand. FiveTwo has raised from under-the-gun, and I have no reason to enter the pot with such a poor hand. Folding is the only sensible play.

K4o is a trash hand with poor playability post-flop. Facing a raise from UTG (FiveTwo, who has shown aggression in prior hands), folding from the small blind is standard. Pot odds aren't compelling enough (need to call 200 into 400 pot for weak equity), and position is poor. Preserve stack for better opportunities early in the tournament.

I’m in the big blind facing an UTG open to 250 with Kh6h (suited). This is a marginal but defendable hand in the BB — suited King with some backdoor flush/straight potential and a King blocker. Calling 150 to win a 400 pot gives ~27% required equity, and versus a typical UTG open my hand has reasonable equity to continue. I’ll call and see a flop, planning to play cautiously out of position: fold to heavy continuation-bet pressure on dry, high-card flops unless I pick up a real piece or a strong draw; if I hit a King, flush draw or other strong draw I’ll pursue value/pressure. If I face a preflop 3-bet I will fold this hand.

I have Kh6h on 4s-8d-9s: no pair, no real draw (only two-card backdoor heart potential), and I'm out of position vs an UTG open. The flop is fairly connected and contains two spades, so the preflop raiser's range often has decent equity or a spade draw. Betting OOP as a bluff here has little fold equity against an UTG raiser and I don't want to invest more with virtually no equity. I'll check to control the pot and fold to any meaningful continuation bet. I will only continue if I pick up a King or a heart (or face a very small probe bet that offers good pot odds).

Heads-up as the preflop raiser, I want to apply pressure with a small c-bet on this 9-8-4 texture. My hand (JcTc) has little showdown value but has strong playability: a double-gutshot (7 or Q) plus backdoor clubs. A small sizing targets folds from A-high/overcards and some underpairs, while keeping my risk controlled if Minni check-raises on this board that connects with their defending range.

Call 200. The bet is small (200 into a 750 pot -> you need ~21% equity), so the price to see a turn is correct. Kh6h has very little immediate value but it has a King blocker, three sixes and three kings as pairing outs and backdoor heart potential — enough equity to continue one street against a small probe from UTG. I’m out of position so raising as a bluff is unattractive; folding would surrender decent pot odds. Plan: call to see the turn and only continue if I hit a King/6 or a heart (or the turn gives a very favorable card/line); fold to significant turn aggression.

I have K♥6♥ on 4♠-8♦-9♠-A♠. I completely missed — backdoor hearts are now dead and my only realistic outs are three kings and three sixes (~6 outs, ~12–13% to pair on the river). The turn A♠ heavily helps the UTG opener’s range (lots of Ax combos and spade holdings), and they were the preflop raiser, so they’re often ahead. Betting out OOP as a bluff is unattractive: it will fold only the weakest parts of their range but will get called by Ax, spade draws, and many hands that beat me, bloating the pot with a marginal holding. I’ll check to control the pot. Plan: fold to any significant bet; only call a very small probe (roughly ≤ ~120 chips) that offers correct pot odds for my ~12% equity. If villain checks back, I’ll re-evaluate the river and only continue with a real pair or a clear, profitable bluffing spot.

Turn A♠ is a strong range card for the UTG preflop raiser (we credibly have lots of Ax, including strong aces), and BB’s check keeps their range capped/indecisive. With J♣T♣ we still have 8 clean outs to the straight (7/Q), so barreling as a semi-bluff is higher EV than checking because we can fold out many 8x/9x/medium pairs and some floats, while also building a pot for when we hit. I’ll use a solid pressure sizing that targets their one-pair region without overcommitting.

Fold. On the turn (4♠-8♦-9♠-A♠) holding K♥6♥ I have only 6 clean outs (3 Ks + 3 6s) ≈ 12–13% to make at showdown. The pot is 1600 and the bet is 650, so the required equity to call is 650/(1600+650) ≈ 29%. My equity (~13%) is well below the pot odds, so a call is -EV. Villain’s line (UTG open, flop bet 200, turn bet 650 after an A♠) strongly represents Ax, a spade, or other made hands — the turn Ace heavily helps their range. I have no meaningful blocker or strong draw and very little fold equity out of position, so bluff-raising is unattractive and risky. I’ll fold and preserve chips for better spots.

I am in UTG position holding 4c 2h, which is effectively the worst starting hand in Texas Hold'em. Being first to act with 4-2 offsuit offers no strategic value; it has poor connectivity, no flush potential, and is easily dominated. There is no justification for entering the pot from early position with this hand. The correct play is to fold immediately.
2s5d is a junk hand in middle position. No suited, no connected cards, very low. Easy fold.

I am in the cutoff position with 9c 4h, which is a very weak starting hand. With several players still to act behind me (Elon on the button, Minni in the SB, and FiveTwo in the BB), there is no reason to attempt a steal or enter the pot with such low equity. Folding is the standard and most profitable play here to preserve my stack.

Preflop on the button with 3sJc offsuit, a very weak hand not in any standard opening range. Despite position and folds to us, this hand has poor playability, low equity, and no strong blockers. Stealing blinds is tempting with deep stacks, but better suited for stronger holdings. Folding preserves stack for better spots early in the tournament.

3♣8♦ offsuit is a very weak, unplayable hand out of the small blind. I’d be out of position for the entire hand and the BB (FiveTwo) has shown willingness to defend and play back, so a raise would likely be called or 3-bet, putting me in difficult multi-street spots with poor equity. Calling 50 to see a flop is also negative EV given my lack of playability and position — the 50 already posted is a sunk cost. With a healthy ~124bb stack and plenty of hands left, I’ll conserve chips and fold.
4c 5s offsuit from UTG is a clear fold. This is a weak hand with poor playability, and being first to act with 5 players behind makes it even worse. No reason to enter the pot with this.

I am holding Queen-Ten of clubs (QTs) in the Middle Position. After UTG folds, this is a standard opening hand in a 6-max game. QTs has great post-flop playability due to its straight and flush potential. I will open for a standard raise of 3x the big blind (300 chips) to take the initiative and potentially thin the field.

Pocket deuces is a speculative hand with good implied odds for set-mining, especially deep-stacked (112 BB effective) and in cutoff position with post-flop initiative. Flash's raise to 300 from middle position is standard; calling gets 1.5:1 direct odds into a 450 pot, but massive implied odds if I hit a set against overpairs or top pair. Folding is too tight preflop in position. 3-betting risks playing OOP if shoved on and reduces implied odds; prefer seeing a cheap flop to realize equity.

I’m on the Button with T9s (T♦9♦). Flash opened to 300 and Elon called; pot is 750 and it costs 300 to see the flop. With ~123bb effective and position, T9s is very playable — good equity and strong implied odds to hit straights/flushes. I prefer calling here rather than 3-betting: a tiny 3-bet won’t fold enough of Flash’s opening range, a larger 3-bet risks unnecessary 4-bets from aggressive players, and calling lets me realize equity and use position postflop. Immediate pot odds (300 into 750) require ~28.6% equity, which T9s can achieve against an open/call range. Plan: call, evaluate the flop, continue with strong pairs or good draws, and fold to heavy aggression if I miss.

We’re in the small blind facing a raise and two cold callers, and we’ll be out of position the whole hand with a dominated suited Ten (T3h). While the pot odds look tempting, this hand realizes equity poorly multiway, is frequently dominated when we make a pair/ten-high flush, and we still have the big blind behind who can squeeze. Best EV is to avoid the marginal spot and preserve stack.

I am in the Big Blind with 7d 5c (75o), facing a 3bb raise from Flash in Middle Position and two calls (Elon in CO, Minni on Button). The pot is 1050 and I need to call 200, giving me very good pot odds of roughly 5.25:1 (requires ~16% equity). However, 75o is a very weak hand that plays poorly post-flop, especially out of position against three active opponents. My equity realization will be low due to the positional disadvantage and the likelihood of being dominated or facing difficult decisions on the flop. Despite the attractive price, the reverse implied odds and difficulty of playing this hand multi-way make folding the disciplined choice to preserve chips.

I have top pair (Queens) on a paired board (Jacks) with a Ten kicker. As the pre-flop aggressor, I should follow up with a continuation bet to extract value from draws (like flush draws or straight draws) and smaller pocket pairs. However, since the board is paired and highly coordinated, I will bet around 40% of the pot to keep the pot manageable in case an opponent is holding a Jack or a stronger Queen. This bet also provides information; a raise from either Elon or Minni would be a strong signal that my top pair is beaten.

Flop misses completely for set-mining with 22; now holding a naked underpair on a paired, draw-heavy board (Jd Jh Qd). Flash's MP raise pre and 38% pot c-bet screams strength: Jx for trips/top two, QJ two pair, QQ top pair, AK draw/bluff rare. Equity <20% vs. range (blocks nothing useful), pot odds 21.6% marginal but poor with reverse implied odds, Minni behind may inflate pot/call raise. Deep stacks but no fold equity for raise, calling invites trouble OOP to Flash's range. Standard fold preserves stack for premium spots.

Flop is Q♦ J♦ J♥; I hold T♦9♦ — a very strong combined draw. I have a four-card diamond flush draw plus an OESD to K/8. Counting unique outs: 9 diamonds + 3 non-diamond Ks + 3 non-diamond 8s = 15 outs → ~31.9% to hit on the turn, ~54.1% to hit by the river. The pot is 1,450 and it costs 400 to call, so immediate pot odds are 400/(1,450+400) ≈ 21.6%. My by-river equity (~54%) well exceeds the required odds, so a call is +EV. I'm in position, so calling preserves implied odds and lets me control the pot and extract value when I hit. Raising as a semi-bluff is an option but here the board is paired and well within Flash’s value range (J/Q/pairs), so raising risks getting committed against made hands (trips/full house) or facing a shove. Plan: call 400, evaluate the turn — if I hit a nut/near-nut draw I'll bet or raise for value; if I miss and face heavy aggression I'll fold.

The turn 2d is a concerning card as it completes a potential diamond flush. My top pair of Queens with a Ten kicker is a solid hand, but the board is now quite dangerous with both a pair of Jacks and three diamonds present. Since Minni called my flop bet, her range likely includes Jacks, flush draws, or potentially a Queen with a better kicker. By checking, I am controlling the pot size and avoiding a difficult spot if I were to bet and face a raise. I want to see how Minni reacts to the flush-completing card before committing more chips.

I have completed a diamond flush (T♦9♦) on Q♦ J♦ J♥ 2♦. This is a very strong hand but vulnerable: only two-card diamond combos with A♦/K♦ beat me, and any Q or J on the river can give an opponent a full house. Flash was the preflop raiser and c-bet the flop, then checked the turn — which often indicates pot control with a one-pair/medium-strength hand or caution because the diamond came in. I want to extract value from worse hands (Qx, medium pairs, single-diamond hands) and charge any draws, while keeping the pot from ballooning into a situation where a full-house check-raise would put me in a tough spot. Therefore I’ll make a value bet of about half the pot (900). If Flash raises large I’ll heavily discount my hand (likely fold or re-evaluate for a fold) because the board texture and his actions make a full house or a superior flush plausible; if he calls I’ll re-evaluate river action and size further value bets depending on the river card.

I have Top Pair (Queens) with a Ten kicker on a board that is now quite dangerous. The turn 2d completes a potential diamond flush, and the board already contains a pair of Jacks. Minni's bet of 900 into a 1850 pot (making the total pot 2750) is roughly 48% of the pot. While I am behind any Jack, any diamond flush, or AQ/KQ, I am getting approximately 3:1 pot odds to call. Given my Top Pair and the possibility that Minni is betting a weaker Queen, a straight draw, or semi-bluffing with a single high diamond, I have enough equity to justify a call. I will call the 900 and evaluate the river action based on the final card.

The river 6h is a complete brick and does not change the board texture. My hand remains two pair (Queens and Jacks) with a Ten kicker. Given that Minni bet the turn when the flush draw completed and I have been in check-call mode since then, I should continue to exercise caution. There are several hands in Minni's range that beat me, including any Jack (trips), any two diamonds (flush), or a Queen with a superior kicker like AQ or KQ. By checking, I am looking to reach a relatively cheap showdown or induce a bluff from a missed straight draw. If Minni bets again, I will have to carefully consider the odds, but for now, checking is the most prudent play to control the pot size.

River: I hold the T♦9♦ flush (T-high diamonds) and the 6♥ is a blank. Pot is 3,650 and Flash checked the river after check‑calling my 900 turn bet. Key points: my flush is strong versus a wide slice of Flash’s likely calling range (Qx, single J, some draws) but is vulnerable to a small set of hands that beat me — specifically any A♦/K♦ diamond combos and full houses (JQ, QQ, or a held J). Flash’s line (open -> c‑bet flop -> check turn -> call 900) looks like medium strength/top pair or a draw that missed rather than an obvious nut flush/full house, but those stronger holdings cannot be discounted. Betting the river can extract value from worse Q/J hands, but it also creates risk of a check‑raise or larger call from a full house/higher flush and turns this into a high‑variance spot for only thin additional value. With about 10k effective stacks and a 3,650 pot, the prudent +EV line is to check behind and take my showdown, avoiding the chance of being raised off a near‑nuts hand. I check to realize my equity and minimize the risk of losing a big pot to the small range of hands that beat me.

I am UTG with 8h 6h in a 6-max game. While suited connectors and gappers have value, opening 86s from the earliest position is generally too loose, especially with aggressive players like Minni and Elon behind me who have shown a willingness to 3-bet and apply pressure. My stack is around 79 BBs, and I prefer to wait for higher-equity hands or better positional opportunities to commit chips.

T3d is a weak suited connector. In middle position with 5 players to act behind (including deep-stacked Minni on cutoff and button), this hand has poor playability and low equity against typical calling or raising ranges. Folding preflop preserves stack for better spots in a long tournament.

Cutoff with Q5 hearts (Q5s) and UTG + Elon folded. With 150 in the pot and only the button and blinds left to act, this is a clear spot to open for value and steal: Q5s has decent postflop playability (queen blocker, backdoor heart/straight potential) and I have position on the button and the blinds. I want a raise size that applies pressure but doesn’t bloat the pot unnecessarily deep-stacks — $300 (3x the BB) is a standard, table-consistent open. Plan: if I face a large 3-bet, I’ll fold and preserve my deep stack; if I get called, I’ll use position to c-bet favorable flops and extract equity with my backdoor draws and top-pair/second-pair potential.

Minni has been opening frequently and showing aggression, so her cutoff range should be fairly wide. On the button, A6o plays awkwardly as a flat (domination issues, and it invites the blinds to come along), but it works well as a light 3-bet: we block strong Ax (AA/AK/AQ), have position, and can often win preflop or take it down with initiative on many flops. If Minni 4-bets, we can comfortably fold. Sizing to 900 pressures her open while keeping our risk controlled in position.

I hold 7h 2h in the Small Blind, which is extremely weak. Facing a raise to 300 from the Cutoff and a 3-bet to 900 from the Button, I am up against significant strength. I am out of position and my hand has negligible equity against these ranges. Cold calling a 3-bet with this hand is not a viable strategy. Folding is the only correct decision.

Holding 5s7d facing a 3-bet to 900. This is a weak offsuit hand with poor playability. I'd need to call 800 into a pot of 1350, getting ~1.7:1 odds. 57o doesn't have the equity or playability to profitably continue against a 3-bet range, especially out of position. Easy fold.

I opened to 300 from the cutoff with Q♥5♥ intending to steal, but the button’s 3-bet to 900 is a sizable, standard 3-bet that puts me out of position for the entire hand. Calling 600 more into a 1350 pot gives ~31% pot odds, but Q5s is a marginal holding that will be frequently dominated by the button’s value combos and is hard to play OOP on later streets. Effective stacks are deep, so a call risks playing a large, difficult pot out of position; a 4-bet is not sensible with this hand. I’ll preserve my deep stack for a better spot and fold here.

UTG position in 6-handed play with K4 offsuit is a standard fold. The hand has poor playability, low equity against calling ranges, and is easily dominated by stronger kings. No suited or connected value to justify entering the pot from early position. Preserve stack for premium hands.

I have A♣? Actually Ah5c — ace-five offsuit — in middle position after UTG folded. With deep stacks (~141 BB) this is a marginal but playable Ace and a good candidate to open. A 2x BB open (to 200 total) takes the initiative, applies pressure to the blinds and gives me fold equity; calling 100 is cheap but passive and invites multiway action where A5o performs poorly. Plan: open-raise to 200; fold to a large 3-bet (or proceed only vs very light 3-bets), and if called proceed post-flop with c-bets on favorable flops (Ace/high boards or when I have strong draws) and play cautiously in multiway pots.

Minni’s 2x open from middle position looks like a wider range, and Kc8c is a reasonable suited blocker hand to attack it in position. Calling can invite a squeeze from the button/blinds and leaves us guessing postflop; a small in-position 3-bet lets us take initiative, win the pot pre often, and when called we still have playability (suited, decent top-pair potential). If Minni 4-bets, we can comfortably fold.

I am on the Button with Ad 5h (A5o). The action began with Minni raising to 200 from Middle Position, followed by a 3-bet to 650 from FiveTwo in the Cutoff. While FiveTwo might be attacking Minni's wide opening range (as seen in Hand 14), facing a raise and a 3-bet with A5 offsuit is a clear fold. The hand is easily dominated by the 3-bettor's value range (better Aces, pairs) and plays poorly post-flop in 3-bet pots. Additionally, Minni is still active behind the 3-bet and could 4-bet. Cold calling is losing, and a 4-bet bluff is too high-risk given the stack depths and stage of the tournament.

Holding 6s3c, one of the worst hands in poker. Facing a raise from Minni and a 3-bet from FiveTwo. This is an easy fold - 63o has terrible equity against raising ranges and no playability postflop. No reason to invest 600 chips with this trash.

I am in the Big Blind with QJo. Minni opened from middle position and FiveTwo 3-bet from the cutoff. QJo is a weak hand to play out of position against a 3-bet, especially when there is still the original raiser left to act. The ranges of both players are likely to dominate my hand (AQ, KQ, AJ, or pairs). Given the stack depths and the action, folding is the most prudent play to preserve my chips.

I opened to 200 as planned and FiveTwo 3-bet to 650 (I must call 450). With A♥5♣ I’m very often dominated by a standard 3-bet range (AK, AQ, medium/big pairs) and my post‑flop playability out of position is poor. Calling costs 450 to win ~1,450 (≈31% pot odds) but my equity vs a reasonable 3‑bet range is unlikely to be that high; a 4‑bet would be a large, polarizing commitment I don’t want to make here. Stick to my preflop plan and preserve the ~13.9k stack for a better spot.

Open-raise to 300 (3x BB) with A♠T♦ from UTG. ATo is borderline from early position in 6-max but it's an Ace with decent postflop playability; I want initiative, to narrow the field, and to avoid a multiway pot out of position. 3x balances fold equity and keeps weaker hands from seeing cheap flops while leaving plenty of stack to maneuver (I have ~139 BB). Table notes: opponents have been willing to call opens and occasionally 3-bet aggressively (Elon, FiveTwo), so I'll fold to a large, polarized 3-bet but can consider calling smaller/isolating 3-bets depending on who's behind. Plan if called: c-bet on dry, Ace-favorable textures, pot-control on wet boards and avoid bloating the pot out of position without a strong made hand.

Facing an UTG open in 6-max, K6s from middle position is too easily dominated by Minni’s value region (better Kx, broadways) and plays poorly if we get squeezed by the four players behind. Calling would often force us into tough postflop spots with marginal top pairs, and 3-betting isn’t a great candidate here given position and reverse-implied-odds. Preserve stack and wait for a better spot.

I hold 8h4h in the Cutoff. Minni has opened to 300 (3bb) from UTG. An UTG opening range is typically strong, often consisting of pocket pairs, strong aces, and broadways. My hand, a low suited two-gapper, is too weak to call profitably against this range, especially with the Button and Blinds still to act behind me who could squeeze. The implied odds are not sufficient to justify a call here given the reverse implied odds if I hit a flush or pair against a stronger range. Folding is the standard and correct play.

4c 6s is a weak offsuit hand. Even with button position, facing a UTG raise from Minni (who has been active), this hand doesn't have good playability or equity to justify calling or 3-betting. Minni has shown aggression and this hand will be difficult to play profitably postflop. Easy fold.

I am in the Small Blind with J4 offsuit. Minni, the chip leader, raised from UTG to 300. J4o is a very weak hand to play out of position against a range that should be relatively tight and strong from early position. There is no reason to defend this hand or attempt a bluff at this stage. I will fold and wait for a better opportunity.

Facing an UTG raise to 300 in a 6-max game with 100BB stacks. My K8d is suited, has decent blockers (Kd blocks AKo/s), and good playability postflop. Vs. a typical 6-max UTG range (88+, AJo+, ATs+, KQs+, QJs), K8s has ~38-42% equity. Pot odds are excellent: calling 200 into 450 (2.25:1), requiring only ~31% equity needed, but postflop realization OOP with suited hand is fine deepstacked. Minni has shown UTG aggression but folded to 3-bets recently (hands 14-15). Folding is too tight; 3-betting polarizes too much with this hand. Calling to realize equity and control pot OOP.

Heads-up pot on a drawy flop (9h8c5c with club flush draw and gutshot possibilities) vs UTG raiser Minni, who has a strong preflop range (88+, AJs+, AQo+). I hold middle pair (eights) with a king kicker (Kd 8d), which has decent equity (~35-45% vs her range, blocking 88/AK) but is vulnerable to overpairs, sets, and better aces. OOP first to act in a 650 pot, deepstacked (~100BB effective). Donk betting risks folding out her weaker pairs/Ax while getting raised by better hands; standard play is to check for pot control, realize equity by check-calling her value/c-bets, and check-raise bluffs/draws from her aggressive line. Backdoor diamond draw adds some playability. Checking keeps her range wide and maximizes EV long-term.

I opened A♠T♦ to 300 and got one caller (Elon). The flop 9♥-8♣-5♣ is fairly dry aside from the two clubs — I have an overcard (A) but no pair or draw. As the preflop aggressor this is a good spot to continuation-bet to pick up the pot and deny equity to club draws and unpaired hands. I’ll make a medium c-bet (~50–60% pot) to preserve fold equity without overcommitting a marginal Ace-high. If I face a large raise I can fold; if I get a single call I’ll re-evaluate on the turn (pot‑control on a blank, proceed cautiously if a club or paired board arrives).

Middle pair with top kicker (K8 on 985cc board) vs UTG raiser Minni's strong range (88+, AJs+, AQo+). Equity ~40% vs her value range, blocking 88/AK. Pot 1000, her 350 bet (35% pot) is small; calling realizes equity OOP with good implied odds deepstacked (~100BB). Pot odds: 350 to call into 1350 (~26% needed). Folding discards too much equity; raising too thin and risks getting coolered by overpairs/sets. Check-call keeps pot controlled, invites bluffs/draws (clubs, 67), and backdoor diamonds add playability. Consistent with preflop plan to realize equity.

On the turn, board is 9h8c5c3d, giving me middle pair top kicker (K8d) with a backdoor diamond flush draw. Pot is 1350, I'm OOP first to act vs Minni's strong UTG range (88+, AJs+, AQo+). Equity remains solid (~38-42% vs range, blocking 88/AK). Turn 3d is relatively blank for her range (misses most Ax overpairs, brings minor gutshots like 64 but unlikely). Donk betting risks folding out her Ax/weak pairs while getting raised by sets/overpairs/combos; not standard OOP. Checking induces bluffs/draws (missed clubs, 67, OESD on flop), allows pot control, and sets up check-call or check-raise vs aggression. Deep effective stacks (~100BB) favor realizing equity passively. Consistent with flop plan to check-call c-bets. Minni's history shows continued aggression postflop when checked to (e.g., Hand 2,8), so expect value/bet from her; calling small bets profitable long-term.

I have A♠T♦ on 9♥-8♣-5♣-3♦. I c-bet the flop and got one caller, and the turn 3♦ is a blank for my hand — I still have only Ace-high with no real draw. With a caller’s range that includes made pairs (9x/8x/5x), club draws and some overcards, I want to keep the pot under control out of position. Checking preserves my showdown equity, lets me call small bets, and avoids bloating the pot with a marginal hand. Plan: check and re-evaluate on the river; call small/medium bets, but fold to large turn aggression.

River pairs the deuce (now 985c33c board) with a club, completing the flush draw and low-end trips possible (33,93s, etc.). I hold middle pair top kicker (K8d), blocking 88/AKs, with ~25-35% equity vs Minni's UTG value range (QQ+,88+,AJ+, sets/combos), but vulnerable to overpairs, sets, 99/55/33 trips, flushes (A/KcQcJcs), and 98/65/43 straight combos. OOP first to act in 1350 pot, deepstacked (~100BB eff with her 13k). Donk betting thin value risks folding out Ax/high cards/draws while getting snapped by better (flush/trips/overpairs); poor EV as her check-back turn caps her checking range somewhat (no nuts). Standard is to check: induces bluffs from missed draws (67,76,44, AcXc without flush), Ax that check-called flop, and showdowns; allows hero-call vs small value if any. Consistent preflop/flop/turn plan to realize equity passively OOP vs strong range. If she bets small, good pot odds to call showdown value; larger bets fold.

Check. I opened A♠T♦ and c‑bet the dry 9♥‑8♣‑5♣ flop to 350 and got one caller — that calling line is consistent with a made pair (9x/8x/5x), a club draw, or a mediocre holding. The 3♦ on the turn was a blank for my hand and both of us checked; the river 3♣ pairs the board and brings a third club. I only have Ace‑high with no club and virtually no river value: a river bet will be called by better hands and only fold out hands I actually beat infrequently. With a deep stack and no need to gamble, I preserve showdown equity by checking; plan is to call small river bets for pot odds but fold to large bets or check‑raises.

6-handed UTG is still an early position spot with several players left who can 3-bet and play well in position. Q8o is dominated by the calling/3-betting ranges (Q9+, QTs+, AQ, KQ, etc.) and plays poorly postflop without the benefit of suitedness/connectivity. With 115bb stacks, there’s no need to force thin opens; I’ll keep the UTG range tighter and avoid tough, negative-EV spots.

I hold Pocket Jacks (Jc Jd) in Middle Position. This is a premium starting hand (Tier 1/Tier 2). With UTG folding, the standard play is to open-raise for value and protection. A raise size of 2.5x BB (250) is consistent with the table's standard opening sizes and my previous play (e.g., Hand 9). This size builds the pot while allowing me to navigate post-flop play reasonably deep-stacked (73.5 BB effective).

A5 suited in the cutoff facing a min-raise from MP. This is a playable hand with position. A5s has good playability - nut flush potential and wheel straight draws. Calling in position is standard here rather than folding or 3-betting.

I am on the button with Ace-Six offsuit. While an Ace is always tempting, Ace-Six is a marginal hand that is frequently dominated by an opponent's stronger Ace (like AK, AQ, or AJ). Pro raised from middle position and Claude called from the cutoff, indicating they both have reasonably strong ranges. If I call, I risk being squeezed by the blinds or hitting an Ace only to lose a large pot to a better kicker. Given the stack depths and the fact that I am in a tournament setting, folding this hand is the most disciplined and profitable play to preserve my stack for better opportunities.

Pocket 3s in SB facing a min-raise from MP (Pro) and a call from CO (Claude). Pot is 650, I've posted SB 50, need 200 more to call the 250 total bet (pot odds ~3.25:1 currently, improving with BB action). Deep stacks (116BB effective vs callers). Small pair good for set-mining multi-way with strong implied odds if BB calls. Equity sufficient vs typical raising/calling ranges (~12% to set-mine viable with >10:1 implied). Folding too tight given odds; raising OOP with 33 not standard. BB (Minni) acts after, may close action or squeeze, but call still +EV. Standard play: call and see flop OOP hoping for set or good board.

Facing a 250 MP open with two callers (CO and SB), the pot is ~850 and I must call 150 more. I hold 4♥6♥ in the big blind — a speculative, suited connector with decent straight/flush potential and good implied odds given deep stacks. Calling is the best option: the immediate pot odds (~15%) justify seeing a flop, raising to isolate is too thin out of position and folding would be too tight. Plan: call to see the flop, fold to a significant preflop 3-bet, and proceed cautiously post-flop — only continue aggressively with clear draws or when I hit meaningful equity.

Preflop, we called a minraise multiway OOP with pocket 3s for set-mining implied odds in a deepstacked tournament spot, which was standard given pot odds and multiway dynamics. Flop 8c Js 2s is a poor board for our hand: two spades create a strong flush draw, jack-high board hits broadway calling/raising ranges, and we have only ~4% equity (2 outs to the set) against typical opponent ranges (Pro's MP raise likely pairs 66+, AQs+, AJs+; callers behind wider but still favoring overpairs/draws). Multiway pot (4 players) with us first to act OOP amplifies fold equity issues for bluffs and strengthens opponents' value/c-bet ranges. No reasonable bet (bluff sizing too thin multiway, value nonexistent), so check. Plan to fold to any bet as our hand has poor playability post-turn (reverse implied odds if we hit set on scary boards) and can't continue profitably against likely aggression from Pro's c-bet range (~50-70% frequency here). Preserves stack for better spots in tournament.
